image_prompt: SVG: Distance / km (0–10) vs Time of day (09:55–10:30), V-shaped line of constant-speed segments falling from 10 km at 09:55 to a minimum 2 km at 10:15 then rising; points every 5 min, reading 8.0 km at 10:00 and 4.0 km at 10:10 (gives 24 km h⁻¹).

render_data.title: Bounce height vs internal air pressure
render_data.prompt: The data points are: (35 kPa, 0.685 m), (40 kPa, 0.72 m), (45 kPa, 0.76 m), (50 kPa, 0.80 m), (55 kPa, 0.83 m). Imagine a straight line of best fit through these points. Using your line of best fit, select the best estimate of the bounce height at a pressure of 55 kPa.

topic: Bounce height vs drop height scatter
image_prompt: SVG: mean-data scatter, x 0.00–4.00 and y 0.00–1.00 (unlabelled axes for student to fill), seven rising points from (0.4,0.12) to (3.2,0.96), with a gap at drop height 1.6 m where the student plots their calculated (1.6, 0.53) point.

render_data.title: Label the axes and place your calculated point
render_data.prompt: The mean-data graph plots bounce height against drop height. Fill each blank from the palette: state the correct label for each axis, and place the data point you calculated in part (b) for the 1.6 m drop height.

slots.label: Calculated point at 1.6 m drop height

topic: Arctic sea ice and global temperature over time
image_prompt: SVG: dual-axis line graph, Arctic sea-ice extent / million km² (8.0–4.0, left) and Temperature / °C (14.0–15.0, right) vs Year (1900–2020); ice extent flat then falls sharply from ~1970 while temperature flat then rises from ~1960 (negative correlation, curves cross ~2000); key for the two curves.
Night cityscape with three coloured firework bursts (yellow, blue, pink) over a silhouetted skyline reflected in water. Labels: 'Light: 3.0 × 10⁸ m s⁻¹', 'Sound: 330 m s⁻¹'.
Night sky with storm cloud above, ground below. Lightning bolt with bright core and blue halo. Dimension line marks 600 000 V between cloud and ground; current arrow alongside marks 20 000 A.

topic: Surge protector wire on a building (lightning rod)
image_prompt: Modern multi-storey office building with rooftop lightning rod. Yellow/green surge-protector wire (insulated) running from rod down the wall to an earth electrode in the ground. Distant lightning bolt approaching the rod. Labels: Lightning rod, Surge protector wire, to earth.

Drag-and-drop palette with α (4/2) and β (0/−1) chips on the left and three decay equations with empty placeholder boxes: Am-241→Np-237+[ ], Po-210→Pb-206+[ ], Np-240→Pu-240+[ ]. Variant palette warm tones.

render_data.title: Identify the particle released in each decay
render_data.prompt: For each decay equation, place it in the bin for the particle it releases. Alpha: mass −4, atomic number −2. Beta: mass unchanged, atomic number +1.

image_prompt: Three-column table: Isotope | Half-life / years | Additional information. Same four isotopes as v1 (Curium-242, Carbon-14, Potassium-40, Uranium-238).
Three-column checkbox grid (Independent / Dependent / Control) × 6 variables: Drop height, Internal air pressure, Bounce height, Size of ball, Bouncing surface, Material of the ball.

render_data.title: Classify the variables
render_data.prompt: Place each variable into its correct category for this basketball-bounce experiment.

image_prompt: Pressure gauge dial 0–120 kPa with inner psi scale 0–18, needle pointing to 75 kPa (between 60 top and 80). Basketball below on a court background. Labels: 'Pressure gauge', 'Basketball'.

topic: Cricket ball + bouncy ball drop animation scene
image_prompt: Indoor scene: man on a ladder holding a cricket ball at 1.6 m, woman with a ruler. Control panel on right with Cricket ball / Bouncy ball headers and three drop heights (1.6/1.2/0.8 m) each with 'Drop ball' buttons; selection arrow at 1.6 m cricket.

topic: Trial data: 1.6 m drop, three repetitions in mixed units
image_prompt: Notebook on a wooden desk: 'Cricket ball bounce experiment'; 1.6 m drop labelled; trial table with Trial 1 0.56 m, Trial 2 52 cm, Trial 3 5.1 × 10² mm. Marker pens beside.
Lined-paper 'My data' notes with five unsorted drop/bounce pairs: 2.4 m → 0.78 m, 2.8 m → 88 cm, 2.0 m → 0.66 m, 120 cm → 36 cm, 8×10¹ cm → 22 cm.
Two players on an indoor racquetball court (blue lines), racquets in hand, blue racquetball in flight.

topic: Racquetball in warm water bath apparatus
image_prompt: Beaker of warm water on an electronic heater with a thermometer; racquetball (blue) submerged. Labels: Warm water, Racquetball, Thermometer, Electronic heater.
Coloured bubbles for transport options (Tram 45 min, Walking 120 min, Bicycle 35 min, Car 18 min, Bus 28 min) with a directional arrow from Sara to a School icon; banner 'Distance: 10 km'.
